other_observers: Cole Wild, Sue Riffe, Dan Laszlo, Eric DeFonso, 4 others of unknown name.; I was aware that Connie Kogler had seen a Gray-cheeked Thrush at CVCG earlier in the day. after viewing her photos, I believe this was a different individual. I think I was the first one in the group to find and identify this bird. Hopefully others w
description: American Robin-like passerine bird; about 25% smaller than a robin; typical size and shape of Catharus species thrush.  Upperparts (tail, rump, wings, back, nape) olive-gray-brown. Crown browner. Dark eye with pale gray orbital. Dark gray auriculars. White throat with black malar stripes/spotting, more spotted toward upper chest. Upper chest dotted with distinct black spots, about 1-2 mm in diameter, becoming gray on the lower chest. Chest background color white, extending to belly and vent. Flanks gray, same color as gray spots on lower chest.  Legs pale pinkish-yellowish. Bill dark except for basal half of lower mandible, which was yellow. Bill typical length of Catharus thrushes.  Underwing with pale buff stripe noticeable in flight.Behaviors: Hopping on ground, perching in small trees near water source.Call: nonePlumage: adult
